content:
- All required gates now pass: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Marked the hard-node-loss task file done with passes=true, ran .ralph/task_switch.sh, and prepared the repo for final commit/push including all .ralph state changes.
- Next I am staging everything, creating the required task-finished commit with test evidence and challenge notes, pushing it, and then quitting immediately.

- Started choose-task flow.
- Read prior progress log; nothing visible needed to carry forward.
- Reading .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md to resolve blockers and apply bug-first precedence.

- Current backlog contains three failing bug tasks and multiple failing non-bug tasks.
- Checking the three bug task files directly for blocked_by and priority tags before selecting.
- Meta-task path is irrelevant unless all unblocked failing tasks are gone, which is not the case from current summary.

- Inspected all open bug task files directly.
- None of the three bug tasks declare blocked_by or explicit priority tags, so they are all unblocked peers.
- Chose rapid-repeated-failovers-can-drop-intermediate-writes as the next task because acknowledged-write loss across rapid promotions is the most severe correctness bug among the open options.

- Wrote selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/bugs/rapid-repeated-failovers-can-drop-intermediate-writes.md.
- Stopping immediately after selection as instructed.

- Read the previous codex progress log and the active Ralph task pointer.
- Confirmed the current task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/rapid-repeated-failovers-can-drop-intermediate-writes.md.
- Next I need to inspect the task contents, write a full detailed plan into the task file, append TO BE VERIFIED, and quit immediately per the task workflow.

- Read the active bug task and traced the concrete code paths in tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs, src/ha/decision.rs, src/ha/decide.rs, src/dcs/state.rs, and the HA docs pages.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into .ralph/tasks/bugs/rapid-repeated-failovers-can-drop-intermediate-writes.md, including the likely root cause, implementation order, unit/E2E coverage, and required docs updates.
- Appended TO BE VERIFIED to the task file and am quitting immediately without executing the plan, per the workflow.

- Re-read the active bug task and performed the required skeptical plan review against the actual HA decision code and repeated-failover E2E path.
- I changed the plan in .ralph/tasks/bugs/rapid-repeated-failovers-can-drop-intermediate-writes.md: the promotion-safety gate must compare against the highest fresh WAL evidence on the candidate timeline, not only a fresh primary record; I also added unit coverage for higher-timeline and replica-only evidence cases, then flipped the task marker to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I should execute the plan directly: add the missing unit tests first, implement the centralized promotion-safety helper and decision wiring, add the dedicated write-survival E2E, update docs with k2-docs-loop, run make check/test/test-long/lint, then mark passes=true, switch task, commit, push, and quit.

- Resuming after context compaction on rapid repeated failover task.
- Need to verify the new failover regression outcome, finish docs via k2-docs-loop, then run make check/test/test-long/lint and close the task if green.

- Targeted repeated failover regression passed after preserving last known WAL evidence in DCS member records.
- Next: update docs with k2-docs-loop, then run make check/test/test-long/lint and close the task if everything stays green.

- All required gates are green on the final tree: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.
- Need to mark the Ralph task as passed, run task_switch.sh, commit all task files and code/docs changes, push, then stop.
